semantic.role: TypeScript compiler configuration for the ArchSpine project
responsibilities: Controls compilation of TypeScript source files into JavaScript output
Defines module system and resolution strategy (NodeNext)
Enforces code quality via strict type checking and consistent file naming
Sets output and root directories for build artifacts
invariants: Strict mode must remain enabled to ensure type safety
forceConsistentCasingInFileNames must be true to prevent cross-platform issues
esModuleInterop must be true for compatibility with CommonJS modules
skipLibCheck must be true to avoid unnecessary checks on third-party libraries

English.role: TypeScript compiler configuration for the ArchSpine project

parameter_definitions.target: ECMAScript target version set to ESNext to leverage latest JavaScript features.
parameter_definitions.module: Module system set to NodeNext (Node.js native ECMAScript module support).
parameter_definitions.moduleResolution: Resolution strategy aligned with NodeNext for modern Node.js compatibility.
parameter_definitions.outDir: Compiled JavaScript output is placed in './dist' to separate source from build artifacts.
parameter_definitions.rootDir: Source files reside in './src'; the compiler uses this to preserve directory structure in output.
parameter_definitions.strict: Enables all strict type-checking options, catching potential runtime errors at compile time.
parameter_definitions.esModuleInterop: Allows default imports from CommonJS modules, simplifying interoperability.
parameter_definitions.skipLibCheck: Skips type checking of declaration files (.d.ts) to speed up compilation.
parameter_definitions.forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: Ensures file name casing matches across imports to avoid issues on case-insensitive filesystems.
parameter_definitions.types: Explicitly includes Node.js type definitions for built-in APIs.
parameter_definitions.sourceMap: Generates source maps for debugging, linking runtime errors back to TypeScript source.

English.stability_and_risks: This configuration ensures predictable, type-safe compilation. Risks are low if settings remain unchanged; altering 'strict' or 'forceConsistentCasing' could introduce subtle bugs. The module system (NodeNext) is critical for correct module resolution in modern Node environments. Skipping lib check (skipLibCheck) is a minor risk but standard practice to avoid slowdowns. Overall, this file is foundational for development stability and should be treated as a critical system component.
